question: Compare Real Madrid and Manchester City Champions League performance over the last 3 seasons.
ground_truth: Real Madrid won the 2023-24 Champions League. Manchester City won 2022-23. Both have been semifinalists or better in most recent seasons.

faithfulness.reasoning: Real Madrid stats and City 2025-26 elimination supported; City 2023-24/2024-25 data missing.

context_precision.reasoning: Chunks 2, 6, 8 show Man City's UCL round of 16 exit; no Real Madrid data.

pipeline.answer:
# Real Madrid vs Manchester City: Champions League Performance (2023–24 to 2025–26)

Over the last three seasons, Real Madrid maintained consistent group-stage excellence and delivered a decisive knockout blow to Manchester City in 2025–26. City, however, can only be assessed for that single campaign due to missing data for 2023–24 and 2024–25.

## Real Madrid: Sustained Dominance

**2023–24 season (first 5 matchdays):** Real Madrid had a perfect record of **5 wins, 0 losses — 15 points**, scoring 13 goals and conceding 5 [3]. This campaign is described as "a dream season where everything was perfect" [3].

**2025–26 season (first 5 matchdays):** A slight dip saw **4 wins, 1 loss — 12 points**, with 12 goals scored and 5 conceded [3]. The defense remained equally stingy.

**Knockout stage (2025–26):** Madrid emphatically eliminated Manchester City in the Round of 16, winning **5–1 on aggregate**, including a **3–0 away win** on 11 March 2026 — City's biggest defeat of the season [2].

### Real Madrid: First 5 Matchdays Comparison

| Metric | 2023–24 | 2025–26 | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Wins | 5 | 4 | [3] |
| Points | 15 | 12 | [3] |
| Goals scored | 13 | 12 | [3] |
| Goals conceded | 5 | 5 | [3] |

## Manchester City: Limited Data, Clear Result in 2025–26

**No source in the provided chunks covers City's Champions League performance in 2023–24 or 2024–25** [1][2][3][4]. One social media post suggests City won only the Community Shield in 2024–25 and failed to qualify for the 2025–26 UCL — a claim **contradicted by the detailed 2025–26 fixture data** [1][2][4].

**2025–26 campaign:** City reached the Round of 16, exiting after a **5–1 aggregate loss to Real Madrid** [2]. Their league phase results included:

| Date | Opponent | Result | Source |
|---|----------|--------|--------|
| 18 Sep 2025 | Napoli (H) | **2–0** win | [2] |
| 1 Oct 2025 | Monaco (A) | **2–2** draw | [1] |
| 21 Oct 2025 | Villarreal (A) | **2–0** win | [1] |
| 5 Nov 2025 | Dortmund (H) | **4–1** win | [1] |
| 25 Nov 2025 | Leverkusen (H) | **0–2** loss | [1] |
| 10 Dec 2025 | Real Madrid (A) | **2–1** win | [1] |
| 28 Jan 2026 | Galatasaray (H) | League phase match | [2] |

City's **lowest attendance** of the season came against Galatasaray at the Etihad (35,259) [2].

## Head-to-Head: The Defining Clash

The two clubs met in the **2025–26 Round of 16**, with Real Madrid winning **5–1 on aggregate**. The second leg at the Bernabéu was City's **biggest defeat of the season** (0–3) [2]. This single tie encapsulates the gulf between the sides in the only season with complete comparative data.

## Key Takeaways

- **Real Madrid's consistency is unmatched**: Perfect group stage in 2023–24 (15 points) and near-perfect in 2025–26 (12 points) [3].
- **Manchester City's data is incomplete**: No source covers their 2023–24 or 2024–25 UCL campaigns; the claim of non-qualification for 2025–26 is unsubstantiated [1][2][3][4].
- **Real Madrid dominated the only direct meeting**: A 5–1 aggregate win in the 2025–26 Round of 16 [2].
- **City's league phase was mixed in 2025–26**: Wins over Real Madrid (A) and Dortmund, but a loss to Leverkusen and a draw with Monaco [1][2].
- **Defensive solidity separates the sides**: Real Madrid conceded just 5 goals in both group-stage comparisons; City's defensive record in 2025–26 is unclear from available data [1][2][3].

urls.title: Men’s Team - Manchester City | Annual Report 2024-25
urls.snippet: In finishing third in the table, Pep Guardiola’s side extended an astonishing run of qualification for the UEFA Champions League. An unbeaten run of ten games towards the end of the Premier League season ensured that City will be competing in the top-tier European club competition for the 15th consecutive time in the 2025-26 season. Manchester City qualified for the Champions League for a 15th consecutive season. The reigning Premier League champions went on to display their class and resilience in the closing weeks to win seven and draw three of the final ten domestic games and rise to third in the standings. In that time the 25-year-old bagged eight Premier League hat-tricks, five more than any other top-flight player in the last three seasons and reached 100 goal involvements (84 goals and 16 assists) after 94 games played, faster than any other player since the Premier League was established.

The 2025–26 season is the 131st season in the existence of Manchester City Football Club and their 24th consecutive season in the topflight of English football. In addition to the domestic league, Manchester City is also participating in the FA Cup, EFL Cup and UEFA Champions League, entering the latter for the 15th consecutive season.
This is the club's first season since 2014–15 without midfielder Kevin De Bruyne, who departed for Napoli following the expiration of his contract, and the first season since 2016–17 without Kyle Walker (to Burnley) and Ederson (to Fenerbahçe).

chunks.title: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025%E2%80%9326_Manchester_City_F.C._season
chunks.chunk_text:
5 without midfielder Kevin De Bruyne, who departed for Napoli following the expiration of his contract, and the first season since 2016–17 without Kyle Walker (to Burnley) and Ederson (to Fenerbahçe). Bernardo Silva was named as the club's new captain after Walker's departure. This also marks the first campaign since 2020–21 which Manchester City did not enter as reigning Premier League champions.
On 17 March, City were beaten 1–5 on aggregate by Real Madrid in the last-16 round of the Champions League to end their participation in this season's competition. On 22 March they won 2–0 against Arsenal to win the EFL Cup for the first time in five seasons. Pep Guardiola became the first manager to win the competition on five occasions.

Season summary
[edit]Pre-season
[edit]The revamped 2025 FIFA Club World Cup spanned the period between the 2024–25 and 2025–26 English football seasons. City's involvement in this competition is documented on their 2024–25 season page.
It had been anticipated that City's squad would need a major overhaul in the summer to remain competitive at the highest level after a trophyless season.

In LaLiga after 13 matchdays:

2023/24: 32 points. 2nd place. 28 goals scored. 9 goals conceded.

2025/26: 32 points. 1st place. 28 goals scored. 12 goals conceded.

In UCL after 5 matchdays:

2023/24: 5 wins. 15 points. 13 goals scored. 5 goals conceded.

2025/26: 4 wins. 12 points. 12 goals scored. 5 goals conceded.

Just one more defeat than in 23/24 and only one goal less scored — while 23/24 is considered a dream season where everything was perfect, some people seriously think Xabi Alonso’s job should be in danger? That’s ridiculous.

chunks.title: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025%E2%80%9326_Manchester_City_F.C._season
chunks.chunk_text:
her key exits included İlkay Gündoğan to Galatasaray and Manuel Akanji on loan to Inter Milan.[14][15]
Following the September 2025 international break, Manchester City recorded consecutive victories. On 14 September, City defeated Manchester United 3–0 at home in the Premier League, with goals from Phil Foden and a brace from Erling Haaland.[16] Four days later, on 18 September 2025, City beat Napoli 2–0 in their opening UEFA Champions League league stage match. Haaland scored his 50th goal in the competition in his 49th appearance, becoming the fastest player to reach the milestone, while Jérémy Doku added the second against the Italian side, who played most of the match with ten men.[17]
Autumn period
[edit]Manchester City resumed their league campaign on 21 September with a 1–1 draw at Arsenal. Erling Haaland put City ahead after nine minutes, but Gabriel Martinelli’s stoppage-time equaliser(90+3) rescued a point for the hosts.[18][19] Three days later, on 24 September 2025, City advanced in the EFL Cup with a 2–0 victory over Huddersfield Town in the third round, as Phil Foden opened the scoring and Savinho doubled the lead.[20][21] This was followed up with an emphatic 5–1 Premier League victory over Burnley on 27 September.

![Image 3: 📊](https://static.xx.fbcdn.net/images/emoji.php/v9/t49/2/16/1f4ca.png) Real Madrid 2023/24 ![Image 4: 🆚](https://static.xx.fbcdn.net/images/emoji.php/v9/t70/2/16/1f19a.png) 2025/26 — You will be surprised!

In LaLiga after 13 matchdays:

2023/24: 32 points. 2nd place. 28 goals scored. 9 goals conceded.

2025/26: 32 points. 1st place. 28 goals scored. 12 goals conceded.

In UCL after 5 matchdays:

2023/24: 5 wins. 15 points. 13 goals scored. 5 goals conceded.

2025/26: 4 wins. 12 points. 12 goals scored. 5 goals conceded.

Just one more defeat than in 23/24 and only one goal less scored — while 23/24 is considered a dream season where everything was perfect, some people seriously think Xabi Alonso’s job should be in danger? That’s ridiculous.
